question_title: Will the interest in “freeze watch” change between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-27 according to Google Trends?
summary: The "freeze watch" search term is currently at value 12 on Mar 17, elevated from a massive spike (peaked at 100 on Mar 15) driven by a "March megastorm" bringing freeze warnings across the southern US. The spike is in rapid decay (100→51→12 over 2 days), following the same pattern as a prior spike on Feb 22 (67→14→3→2→1, back to baseline in 3 days). By Mar 27, 10 days out, the value will almost certainly be at baseline (1-3), well below the threshold of 9 needed for "Decreases." The current cold snap is ending (NWS forecasts warming to 70s-80s by mid-week), and late March past the spring equinox makes new freeze events unlikely. The only scenario preventing "Decreases" is another significant freeze event near Mar 27, which is possible but unlikely given seasonality.

factors.description: Current value of 12 is elevated from massive spike (peaked at 100 on Mar 15). Prior spike (Feb 22, peaked 67) decayed to baseline in 3 days (67→14→3→2→1). Current spike shows similar rapid decay (100→51→12). By Mar 27, value should be at baseline (1-3), well below threshold of 9.
Seasonal factor: Late March past spring equinox. 12-month data shows freeze watch interest drops to 1 by April. New spike near Mar 27 unlikely.
News confirms current freeze/megastorm ending quickly. NWS forecasts warming to 70s-80s by mid-week (Mar 18-19). Search trigger is dissipating.

Weather unpredictability: another freeze event could occur within 10-day window. Would need to push Mar 27 value above 9 (Doesn't change) or 15 (Increases) - significant threshold given late-season timing.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty (±1-3 points). At expected end value of 1-3, this cannot bridge the gap to 9 threshold.

reasoning:
**Forecast: "freeze watch" Google Trends change, Mar 17–27**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| **Decreases** | **80%** |
| Doesn't change | 12% |
| Increases | 8% |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Post-spike decay is the dominant dynamic.** The term spiked to 100 on Mar 15 from a "March megastorm" and has already dropped to 12 by Mar 17. The Feb 22 precedent spike (67→14→3→2→1) shows complete return to baseline in 3 days. With 10 days until Mar 27, baseline (1-3) is virtually certain absent a new event.

- **Threshold arithmetic strongly favors Decreases.** The start value is 12; "Decreases" requires end value <9. Expected end value of 1-3 is well below 9.

- **Seasonality reduces new-event risk.** Late March is past the spring equinox, and 12-month data shows interest drops to 1 by April. The current cold snap is ending per NWS forecasts (warming to 70s-80s by mid-week).

- **Residual uncertainty** comes from the possibility of an unexpected late-March freeze event near Mar 27, plus minor SerpAPI measurement differences.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=freeze%20watch&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=freeze%20watch&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27) for 2026-03-27 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-17. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "freeze watch",
  "date": "2026-02-25 2026-03-27",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
